news.headline: Incent receives investment from Tyfone to boost youth digital banking solutions, expand capabilities, and empower next generation.

news.summary: The ASPIRe strategy, comprised of five pillars, is designed to address challenges such as liquidity fragmentation, regulatory arbitrage, and market volatility. This strategy also seeks to promote innovation within the blockchain sector.

news.headline: Australian miner MinRes stocks plummet 22.1% due to EBITDA falling 55% to $191 million, resulting in a loss of A$807 million.
news.summary: Australian mining company Mineral Resources (MinRes) (OTC:MALRF) experienced its most significant drop in stock value since the Great Recession, with shares plummeting by 22.1% in Australia. This decline erased nearly five years' worth of stock appreciation. The stock's downward trajectory was spurred by a substantial decrease in EBITDA and a reduction in the fiscal 2025 production forecast. The company's adjusted EBITDA saw a 55% decline to around $191 million (A$302 million), compared to approximately $428.5 million (A$675 million) the previous year. Net profit after tax also took a hit, transitioning from A$530 million in the first half of fiscal 2024 to a loss of A$807 million. This loss included a post-tax impairment charge of A$352 million linked to the Bald Hill lithium mine and an A$232 million foreign exchange impairment. Mineral Resources encountered challenges across its divisions, with Mining Services being the sole positive performer in this period.

news.headline: Global cochlear implants market valued at US$2.58 billion in 2023, set to reach US$4.73 billion by 2030 with 9.2% CAGR driven by factors like rising hearing loss prevalence. Market segmented into two components, two implant types, patient types, and end-users. Bilateral implants and adults segments expected to witness highest growth. Market opportunities in emerging economies for developing low-cost implants and telemedicine integration. Market driven by aging population, cognitive impacts of hearing loss awareness, and improved access to services.
news.summary: The global cochlear implants market was valued at US$2.58 Billion in 2023 and is expected to grow at a CAGR of 9.2%, reaching US$2.80 Billion in 2024 and US$4.73 Billion by 2030. Factors driving this growth include the increasing prevalence of hearing loss, especially among the elderly, and growing awareness of hearing rehabilitation options. The market also benefits from advancements in AI-enabled audio processing, pediatric implantations for speech development, and improved surgical techniques. Emerging economies offer opportunities for developing low-cost implants and integrating telemedicine for post-implant care. Cochlear implants consist of external and internal components and are used to provide a sense of sound for individuals with profound hearing loss. The market is segmented based on component type, with cochlear implant systems expected to have the highest growth rate. Unilateral and bilateral implants are the two types of implants available, with the latter anticipated to witness the highest growth due to clinical benefits such as better sound localization and speech recognition. The market is also segmented by patient type into adults and pediatric, with the adult segment expected to grow rapidly due to the increasing prevalence of age-related hearing loss and technological advancements. Additionally, the market is divided by end-users into hospitals, ENT clinics, and other users, with the hospital segment predicted to expand significantly between 2024 and 2030.

news.headline: Global shockwave therapy devices market valued at $1,832.6M in 2024, projected to reach $3,376M by 2034 with 6.3% CAGR. North America expected to have 24.3% market share in 2034. Radial shockwave therapy devices to grow at 6.2% CAGR with $876.2M opportunity from 2024 to 2034. Key players: BTL, Enovis Corporation.
news.summary: The global shockwave therapy devices market, valued at US$1,832.6 million in 2024, is projected to grow at a CAGR of 6.3% to reach US$3,376.0 million by 2034. Shockwave therapy machines provide non-surgical treatments using acoustic waves, benefiting conditions like musculoskeletal disorders and chronic pain. Market players like BTL and Enovis Corporation are leading in this sector. Radial shockwave therapy devices are estimated to grow at a CAGR of 6.2%, with North America expected to hold a market share of 24.3% in 2034. The market saw an opportunity of US$395.1 million between 2019 to 2023. Increasing adoption in sports rehabilitation and aesthetic treatments, along with technological advancements, are driving market growth. North America and East Asia are forecasted to create an absolute $ opportunity of US$751.7 million collectively.

The company's total revenue growth guidance for 2025 is projected to be between 17-24%. Galafold net product sales in 2024 were $458.1 million, up 18% year-over-year. Pombiliti + Opfolda net product sales were $70.2 million in 2024, reflecting a significant increase. The company aims to exceed $1 billion in total sales by 2028. Their non-GAAP operating expenses for 2024 were $347.8 million. Cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities totaled $249.9 million at the end of 2024. news.headline: OGE Energy Corp. reported earnings of $2.19 per diluted share in 2024 (compared to $2.07 in 2023) with net income of $441.5 million. OG&E had earnings of $2.33 per diluted share in 2024. The Company forecasts a consolidated earnings per share growth rate of 5-7% for 2025.
news.summary: OGE Energy Corp. reported earnings of $2.19 per diluted share in 2024, increased from $2.07 in 2023. OG&E, a segment of OGE, contributed $2.33 per diluted share, up from $2.12 in 2023, while other operations reflected a loss of $0.14 per diluted share, compared to 2023's loss of $0.05. The net income for OG&E in 2024 was $469.9 million, up from $426.4 million in 2023. Overall, OGE Energy's net income for 2024 was $441.5 million or $2.19 per diluted share, compared to $416.8 million or $2.07 in 2023. For the fourth quarter of 2024, OGE Energy reported net income of $101.9 million, an increase from $48.2 million in the same period of 2023. The company expects consolidated earnings per share growth rate between 5-7% annually from the midpoint of the 2025 guidance range of $2.27 per average diluted share. OG&E, with approximately 907,000 customers, declared a second-quarter dividend of $0.42125, payable on April 25, 2025. The 2025 financial results guidance and further details are available in OGE Energy Corp.'s Form 10-K. Some matters discussed in the article contain "forward-looking statements" that are subject to risks and uncertainties. Factors affecting OGE's results include economic conditions, operational and regulatory factors, environmental laws, and more. The Company will be hosting a conference call on February 19, 2025.

news.headline: Stepan reported Q4 net income of $3.4M, up from a $1.2M loss. Adjusted EBITDA was $35.8M, up 39% YoY. YTD net income was $50.4M, up 25%. Global sales volume increased 1%. Polymer net sales decreased by 12%, while Specialty Products saw a 10% increase. Company delivered $13M in cost savings in Q4. CEO mentioned strategic investments and steps to return to growth. Positivity for future growth in the Surfactant business. EBITDA expected to improve in all reporting segments in 2025. Surfactant operating income grew by 9% in the quarter. Polymers saw a 73% decrease in operating income due to demand shortfall. Specialty Products saw a 102% increase in operating income.公司经历了2024年的整体财务表现并不理想, 但取得了一些进展, 政策，以及为未来增长提供的机会。
news.summary: Stepan Company reported a net income of $3.4 million in the fourth quarter of 2024 as compared to a $1.2 million net loss in the previous year. Adjusted net income was $2.8 million, down 63% from the prior year. EBITDA was $35.8 million, up 39%, and Adjusted EBITDA was $35.0 million, down 7% year-over-year. Global sales volume decreased by 1% year-over-year. Cash from Operations was $68.3 million, and free cash flow for the quarter was $32.1 million. For the full year, reported net income was $50.4 million, up 25% from the previous year. Adjusted net income was flat, and EBITDA and Adjusted EBITDA increased by 13% and 4% respectively year-over-year. The company also delivered cost out savings of $13.0 million in the fourth quarter of 2024. CEO Luis E. Rojo expressed disappointment in the financial performance in 2024 but emphasized strategic investments for future growth. Surfactants and Specialty Products showed strong growth, while Polymers experienced demand weakness. Stepan is optimistic about 2025, focusing on growth strategies, cost reductions, and delivery of improved financial performance.
